name: Embalse del Teliarán — loop from Calañas
name_original: Embalse del Teliarán — bucle desde Calañas
region_intro: Hiking around Calañas offers diverse landscapes in the heart of Huelva's Andévalo region, characterized by a mix of plains and the rolling foothills of the Sierra de Aracena. The area features extensive dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, along with significant water resources like the River Odiel. Its unique geological features, shaped by a rich mining history, contribute to a distinctive scenery ideal for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Calañas The most popular hiking route is Circular route along the Odiel River, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 54 minutes to complete, following the river's unique chromatic variations. Another top favourite among local hikers is Odiel Mills River Trail, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.8 km) path. This route highlights Calañas's topography and historical elements, including old mills and mining remnants. Local hikers also love the Embalse del Calabazar – circular from La Coronada Hermitage, an 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Calabazar reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Calañas is defined by river valleys, dehesas of holm and cork oaks, and landscapes shaped by historical mining. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Calañas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Calañas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Calañas?
A: Calañas offers a good selection of routes, with over 10 hiking trails recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging excursions.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Calañas?
A: The region around Calañas is characterized by a diverse mix of landscapes. You'll find expansive plains, rolling foothills leading to the Sierra de Aracena, and extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; of holm and cork oaks. The area is also shaped by its rich mining history and features significant water resources like the River Odiel, which adds unique chromatic variations to the scenery.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Calañas has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131714050/corta-de-los-silos-y-el-embalse-de-puerto-leon-circular-desde-la-zarza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta de los Silos y el embalse de Puerto León – circular desde La Zarza&lt;/a&gt; is rated as easy and offers a pleasant experience through the local landscape.

Q: What unique natural features can I discover on the trails?
A: Hikers can experience the striking chromatic variations of the River Odiel, whose waters are stained red by dissolved iron and can also exhibit green hues. The Barranco de Peñas Pardas (Brown Rocks Ravine) is another impressive feature, where the Odiel River carves through robust stone, showcasing red-stained rocks and unique vegetation like the endemic Andevalo heather (&lt;em&gt;Erica andevalensis&lt;/em&gt;).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Calañas?
A: Yes, many routes in Calañas are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984001616/circular-route-along-the-odiel-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular route along the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 6.4-mile (10.3 km) trail following the river's unique features. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984005998/embalse-del-calabazar-circular-from-la-coronada-hermitage" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Calabazar – circular from La Coronada Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, an 9.0-mile (14.4 km) trail around the Calabazar reservoir.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I explore on the trails?
A: The trails around Calañas offer a glimpse into the region's history and natural beauty. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge over the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de La Coronada&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdelamusa Water-Filled Quarry&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5733544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Mills Trail&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, showcasing historical elements like old mills and mining remnants.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Calañas?
A: The hiking routes in Calañas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique river landscapes and the blend of natural and historical sights.

Q: Are there any longer hiking routes for more experienced hikers?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984005998/embalse-del-calabazar-circular-from-la-coronada-hermitage" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Calabazar – circular from La Coronada Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, covering 9.0 miles (14.4 km) with moderate difficulty. Another substantial route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131741495/campanario-reservoir-via-verde-de-los-molinos-del-agua-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campanario Reservoir - Vía Verde de los Molinos del Agua Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.0 miles (12.9 km) long and also rated moderate.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Calañas?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, the general natural environment of Calañas, including areas bordering the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park, is often welcoming to dogs, especially on leash. It's always advisable to check local signage and be respectful of wildlife and other hikers.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of hiking in Calañas?
A: Hiking in Calañas offers a range of difficulties. Out of the 12 available tours, 3 are rated as easy, 8 as moderate, and 1 as difficult. This variety ensures that both casual walkers and more experienced hikers can find suitable routes.

Q: Are there any routes that highlight the mining history of Calañas?
A: Yes, the region's mining history is an integral part of its landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1131649516/odiel-mills-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Odiel Mills River Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, highlighting Calañas's topography and historical elements, including old mills and mining remnants like the Pedro José manganese mine.

Q: What other outdoor activities are available in the Calañas area besides hiking?
A: Beyond hiking, Calañas provides opportunities for other adventurous pursuits. Visitors can experience a via ferrata or enjoy water activities like kayaking and sailing on the Calabazar reservoir and along sections of the River Odiel.
tips: One of the emblems of the town of Calañas dating from the 20th century and located in the center of town.

Its history dates back to the 16th century, and some original parts, such as the ribbed vaults and the doorway at the foot, date back to the time of the Catholic Monarchs. Over the years, extensions and modifications were made, including the replacement of the roof in the 19th century. During the Civil War, the church suffered damage to its artistic heritage. I hope you enjoy exploring this beautiful place of worship! 🙏⛪
